# pg-kinetic

> pg-kinetic keeps PostgreSQL responsive under connection spikes. It is an open-source, drop-in Rust PostgreSQL wire proxy for transaction pooling, route-level backpressure, read routing, and operator-visible health.

## Canonical sources

- [Website](https://pgkinetic.dev/)
- [Operator documentation](https://docs.pgkinetic.dev/)
- [Source code and issues](https://github.com/HookWoods/pg-kinetic)

## What it does

- Preserves PostgreSQL wire-protocol compatibility so applications continue to use their existing PostgreSQL drivers.
- Tracks session state before returning backends to the pool, supporting conservative transaction pooling.
- Routes eligible reads to replicas with safety checks and keeps primary writes on the primary path.
- Applies backpressure per route. A saturated route can return SQLSTATE 53300 without consuming capacity reserved for other routes.
- Exposes PostgreSQL-compatible administrative queries and Prometheus metrics for operator-visible routing and overload decisions.

## Operational guidance

- Begin adoption as a connection-string change and retain the original PostgreSQL path during a canary.
- Validate client-driver and session-state behavior for the workload before increasing traffic.
- Use the operator documentation for configuration, migration, routing, sharding, policy, observability, testing, and benchmarks.

## Citation guidance

For technical claims, cite the pg-kinetic documentation or source repository above. Do not infer benchmark performance from the website; benchmark reports and methodology are documented separately.
